tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] PER with FG test with AGC with ECC + CRC.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana plans to take QS127M into production. 

Deliverable:  Calculate the Packet Error Rate (PER) for all modes of operations for QS127M.
Plot the PMDR plots.

Risks (T,E):

Limitations (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency (D))

Results 

(T)iger is a risk that everybody understands
(E)lephant is a risk that I know but others don't think so, yet

comments.text: Task is completed. Debug activities are tracked as separate tasks. Result: Sensitivity is in line with QS127S measurements.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Thanks @Vinayak Agrawal  . Extending the due date to Wed.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: All the optimisations were finished on Saturday. I had updated the presets on my branch and was comparing performances, but this step didn't finish because the sweep got stuck at some point. I will have to run it again overnight, and will be able to close the task only after that
comments.author: Vinayak Agrawal

tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: Optimisation step got stuck during sweep and couldn't be completed.
2. Technical: Sweep process encountered a technical issue that prevented completion of optimisations.

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] Baseband schedule bidirectional test.

comments.text: @Pushpal Das  Delay reason : Task got delayed because of few unforeseen issues (ide slot related issues) and debug took longer than anticipated. Needed to take help from firmware team to fix the issues and hence delayed in completing the task.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula

Test Results:
This task can now be marked as completed. You can find the detailed test results here

Packet Data Rate (DR) 266k, 1M: 
Packet Data Rate (DR) 3p33M, 5M:

Test Environment & Setup
The testing was divided into two primary hardware configurations based on the packet data rate (DR). In all cases, the data rates for the ACK and Data packets were kept identical.

undefinedExceptions: Configurations Requiring an Idle Slot
While most configurations ran without an idle slot, the following two specific cases required one to maintain stability and achieve PMDR = 0:

undefinedTested Configurations Summary
We successfully achieved a Packet Missed Detection Rate (PMDR) of 0 for almost all configurations. The table below outlines the full scope of settings covered:

undefined
---
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text:
Task Update:
We have identified a firmware bug, which is now being tracked under this task : 86e0t2m5j
comments.author: Anish Saha

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] RO frequency calibration test.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana plans to take QS127M into production. 

**Deliverable:** 
The following test is used to measure the digitized Ring oscillator frequency. The reference is **schedule clock (2.5MHz)**.
**Measured Parameters:** Status burst read 2 locations from 0x0462 for **ro\_cal\_done**, **cycle\_count\_ro\_cal\[14:8\], cycle\_count\_ro\_cal\[7:0\]**
**Expectation:** Calculated RO clk frequency should be **~32kHz**.

Detailed description of the test is documented here: See #16 See RO frequency calibration.
[https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-74597](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-74597)

**Risks (T,E):**

**Limitations (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency (D))**
Assumption: FW is working as expected.

Results 

(T)iger is a risk that everybody understands
(E)lephant is a risk that I know but others don't think so, yet

comments.text: Delay reason: The deliverable understanding from the point of arc and design, it took time hence delayed. It should've been done earlier, later we got an agreement based on the design feedback that whatever we measure was correct.
comments.author: Pushpal Das

comments.text:
To be clear.
This INV_based RO is used to detect the process corner of a device (fast, slow, typical).

based on the results captured the RO code is working as expected. Since we don't have any targeted process skew material yet to determine its entire functionality - for now we mark this task as complete based on the results obtained for one device.

If and when we get the targeted skews - we will verify this RO codes to determine its full functionality.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: @Anish Saha - thanks .. but it says that the RO_clk is at 357 KHz while expectation is 32KHz  ? Am i missing some thing here ?
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula

PPT Link :
Here is the required calculations:

image.png
image.png
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: @Anish Saha  - For RO - test  the expectation is to show the calculated RO Clk freq is 32 KHz .. where is that calculation. I have only a plot of code ?
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula  These are the test results we can close this task.
image.png
comments.author: Anish Saha

tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: Deliverable understanding took time due to design feedback.
2. Technical: Assumption of FW working as expected was not validated.

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] FIFO read write test - 100K with varying FIFO sizes (256, 512, 1024).

comments.text: Delay reason : there was a couple of issues surfaced during the post silicon checkout. next steps to identify  on the path forward as a startegy took time and we have to update the scripts and redo the data collection and hence delayed.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Thanks @Anish Saha  for the note. As discussed in the teams chat, lets proceed with flushing the FIFO after the FIFO change and share the results here.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Task Update:  We have found some issues in the test; we are waiting for the stakeholders to get a final decision on the solution.
We had a discussion with Sayantan regarding the FIFO read/write failures, and we identified the root cause. The issue occurs because the FIFO size is being changed dynamically without flushing the FIFO beforehand. As a result, the FIFO pointers may end up pointing to undefined locations when the size changes.
To avoid this, we may need to flush the FIFO before starting the FIFO read/write rollover test.
There are two possible solutions:
1. Flush the FIFO before the start of each run — This would ensure the pointers are reset, although it may be somewhat excessive.
2. Change the FIFO size dynamically in increasing order (e.g., 256 → 512 → 1024) — In this case, the pointers are less likely to enter an invalid state since the FIFO size is expanding rather than shrinking. ( Note previously I was doing this in opposite order that's why we run into this problem first place.)
Note: In practical scenarios, the FIFO size would typically remain constant. Even if it does change, the FIFO would generally be flushed before modifying the size.
comments.author: Anish Saha

tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: Post-silicon checkout issues surfaced during test execution.
2. Technical: Insufficient detail in thread regarding root cause of FIFO read/write failures and required solution.

Task Update:  We have found some issues in the test; we are waiting for the stakeholders to get a final decision on the solution.
We had a discussion with Sayantan regarding the FIFO read/write failures, and we identified the root cause. The issue occurs because the FIFO size is being changed dynamically without flushing the FIFO beforehand. As a result, the FIFO pointers may end up pointing to undefined locations when the size changes.
To avoid this, we may need to flush the FIFO before starting the FIFO read/write rollover test.
There are two possible solutions:
1. Flush the FIFO before the start of each run — This would ensure the pointers are reset, although it may be somewhat excessive.
2. Change the FIFO size dynamically in increasing order (e.g., 256 → 512 → 1024) — In this case, the pointers are less likely to enter an invalid state since the FIFO size is expanding rather than shrinking. ( Note previously I was doing this in opposite order that's why we run into this problem first place.)
Note: In practical scenarios, the FIFO size would typically remain constant. Even if it does change, the FIFO would generally be flushed before modifying the size.
comments.author: Anish Saha

comments.text: Delay Reason : During the power on activities - as Deepthi was busy with other things - it was decided to change the ownership to Anish on thursday. On thursday - it took some time for Anish to get to speed on the task (Anish is aware of the FIFO read write and Deepthi is familiar with the external clock) . Hence the data collection took time and the data collection is completed 2 days later.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula
Anish Saha

comments.text: Changed the ownership to@Anish Saha
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Hi @Sarat Anumula, this task couldn't be targeted today due to the TX carrier measurements task. As we defined the FFT captures and the drive strength sweep for the TX task only on Saturday evening, test cases have increased by more than 80x - across 7 different drive strengths, across 4 carriers across 3 Vbat voltages for both FFT and signal capture.

As the TX test data is important for the wearables and regulatory testing, I will come back to this Flash PLL ADC/FIFO read-write task once after the TX carrier task is complete

Hence, can you please change the due date to Thursday. Thank you.
comments.author: Deepthi Kammath

comments.text:
New patch essentially made changes to FW to be able to accurately capture teh GUI settings. Earlier the GUI settings are not being properly taking effect with FW.

The new modified FW is in pre_release.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Thanks @Anish Saha

With the new patch provided by Ethan - PMDR is zero across all modes of baseband transmission
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
@Sarat Anumula
This Task can be marked as completed.
Test Results: 

Here is a summary of the configurations tested. For all listed tests, the SPI Frequency was 9.6M, VDD_AON was 750 mV, and SYS_CLK was 5M, NO IDLE SLOT

Tested Configurations Summary
The tests utilized a combination of CRC, ECC, and DC Balance settings across various datarates and data sizes.
We are getting pmdr = 0 for all configs.


undefined
comments.author: Anish Saha

We have identified a firmware bug, which is now being tracked under this task : 86e0t2m5j
